_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL
name: Aether Tickets
author: aether@example.com
description: Aether Tickets v1.7 — Discord ticket bot with Web Dashboard. Features: ticket creation, claiming, auto-close, chat sync (Discord ↔ Web UI), per-server categories, and more. Auto-clones from GitHub on install, auto-pulls updates on every restart. No file uploads needed.

installation.script:
#!/bin/sh
# ============================================================
# Aether Tickets — Pterodactyl installation script
# Runs ONCE when the server is first created.
# ============================================================
apk add --no-cache git curl python3 py3-pip

cd /mnt/server

GIT_REPO="${GIT_REPO:-https://github.com/Shaf2665/Aether_Tickets.git}"
GIT_BRANCH="${GIT_BRANCH:-main}"

echo "[install] Cloning ${GIT_REPO} (branch: ${GIT_BRANCH})..."
git clone --depth=1 --branch "${GIT_BRANCH}" "${GIT_REPO}" .

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[install] ERROR: git clone failed. Check GIT_REPO and GIT_BRANCH."
  exit 1
fi

echo "[install] Installing Python dependencies..."
pip3 install -U pip --quiet
pip3 install -U -r requirements.txt --quiet

echo ""
echo "============================================================"
echo " Installation complete!"
echo "============================================================"
echo " Next steps:"
echo "  1. Go to the Startup tab and fill in your Discord Bot Token"
echo "  2. Click START -- the bot will launch automatically"
echo "  3. In your Discord server, run: /setup start"
echo "============================================================"

variables.name: 📖 QUICK SETUP GUIDE — READ FIRST
variables.description: STEP 1 — Create your Discord bot: Go to https://discord.com/developers/applications → New Application → Bot tab → Reset Token → copy the token and paste it into 'Discord Bot Token' below. Enable all 3 Privileged Gateway Intents (Presence, Server Members, Message Content). | STEP 2 — Invite the bot to your server: In the Developer Portal → OAuth2 → URL Generator. Tick scopes: bot + applications.commands. Tick permissions: Manage Channels, Manage Roles, Read Messages, Send Messages, Embed Links, Read Message History, Manage Messages. Open the generated URL and invite the bot. | STEP 3 — Fill in 'Discord Bot Token' below, then click SAVE and START the server. | STEP 4 — In your Discord server, run the slash command: /setup start — and follow the prompts to configure the ticket panel. | OPTIONAL — Enable auto-close: run /autoclose set <hours> to automatically close inactive tickets (e.g. /autoclose set 24). Use /autoclose status to check the current setting. | That's it! The bot is ready.

variables.name: 🔴 REQUIRED — Discord Bot Token
variables.description: Paste your Discord bot token here. Get it from: https://discord.com/developers/applications → select your app → Bot → Reset Token. WARNING: Never share this token with anyone.

variables.name: 🟡 OPTIONAL — Guild ID (faster slash command sync)
variables.description: Your Discord server ID. If set, slash commands appear instantly in that server. Without it, commands may take up to 1 hour to appear globally. To get your server ID: enable Developer Mode in Discord (User Settings → Advanced → Developer Mode), then right-click your server name → Copy Server ID.

variables.name: Auto Update on Restart
variables.description: Automatically pull the latest code from GitHub every time the server restarts. Enabled = always update (recommended). Disabled = keep current files.

variables.name: ━━━━━━━━━━ 🔧 ADVANCED (do not change unless you know what you're doing) ━━━━━━━━━━
variables.description: These are pre-filled with correct defaults. Only change them if you have forked the repository or need a custom setup.

variables.name: Git Repository URL
variables.description: The GitHub repository to clone and pull updates from. Pre-filled with the official Aether Tickets repo. Only change this if you have forked the repository.

variables.name: Git Branch
variables.description: Which branch to track. 'main' = stable releases. Only change if you want to run a development/beta branch.

variables.name: Database Path
variables.description: Path to the SQLite database file. The default 'tickets.db' saves it in /home/container which is persistent across restarts. Do not change this.
